title: re:Invent re:Cap - Tampa
location: WeWork Building - Downtown Tampa, 501 E Kennedy Blvd Suite Floor 2, Tampa, FL 33602
description: Join us for the re:Cap event series. Get a comprehensive summary of key re:Invent 2025 announcements and insights from the keynotes, followed by three deep dive sessions selected from the most popular re:Invent presentations covering Amazon Bedrock, AgentCore, Kiro, and Amazon Quick Suite.

title: AI-Assisted Spring Boot App Development & Deployment to Heroku and AWS
location: Entrepreneur Collaborative Center (ECC), 2101 E. Palm Avenue, Tampa, FL
description: Build a Spring Boot app in minutes using AI. Deploy to Heroku (easy & fast) and AWS (scalable & production-ready). Compare real-world DevOps workflows, add AI-powered endpoints inside your app, and use AI for debugging, configs, and CI/CD. Includes live demo, free GitHub template, and Q&A.

title: How the PGA Tour is Leveraging AWS and GenAI
location: Embarc Collective, 802 E. Whiting St., Tampa, FL
description: A high-impact networking event for local tech professionals and AWS enthusiasts. Join us for an exclusive session co-presented by the PGA TOUR and NLP Logix, exploring how Gen AI and AWS are transforming the fan experience through real-time play-by-play commentary and more.

title: Hands-on Workshop: Implementing Amazon Nova Foundation Models
location: Embarc Collective, 802 E. Whiting St., Tampa, FL
description: An immersive, hands-on workshop exploring Amazon's Nova foundation models. Participants will get direct experience with Nova Micro, Lite, Pro, Nova Canvas, and Reel through Amazon Bedrock, covering text, image, and video generation, prompt engineering best practices, and real-world implementation patterns.

title: Elevating Disaster Recovery for Today's Cybersecurity Threats
location: Online
description: Jetsweep and Elastio explore best practices for ransomware protection and disaster recovery in cloud environments, including data integrity validation, zero-trust recovery models, and a real-world ransomware case study.

title: From Complexity to Clarity: An AWS Security Journey
location: Hays, 4350 W Cypress St #1000, Tampa, FL
description: Dhiren Zakkam, Manager for Cloud Engineering at World Kinect, shares his real-world experience managing identities, permissions, and access in AWS at scale — what worked, what didn't, and how his team streamlined their AWS security strategy.

title: Building a GenAI Workflow using Amazon Bedrock
location: 501 E Kennedy Blvd, Tampa, FL
description: A hybrid presentation led by Ricky Brar (Co-Founder, Data Mastery) covering Amazon Bedrock, prompt templates, prompt flows, knowledge bases, and a live demo building a conversational GenAI workflow.

title: AWS re:Invent Recap
location: 501 E Kennedy Blvd, Tampa, FL
description: Local Solution Architects Renato Fichmann and Vivek Mittal from AWS discuss announcements and highlights from re:Invent 2024. Sponsored by JetSweep with refreshments served.

title: AWS re:Invent 2024 Roadshow
location: Tampa, FL (location TBD)
description: AWS in-person re:Invent roadshow covering GenAI data foundations, scalable RAG with Amazon Bedrock, DevOps and Q Developer productivity, and more. Note: RSVPs via aws-experience.com.

title: GenAI on AWS: Use Cases, Challenges, and Solutions
location: 501 E Kennedy Blvd, Tampa, FL
description: Cerebral Blue presents real-world GenAI use cases on AWS leveraging Amazon Bedrock, Lambda, Elasticsearch, Lex, Kendra, Textract, and external data sources. Rescheduled due to tropical storm.

title: AWS Observability: Monitoring with Open-Source Tools
location: Online
description: A session on AWS managed open-source observability tools including Amazon Managed Service for Prometheus, Amazon OpenSearch Service, and Amazon Managed Grafana for monitoring containerized environments.

title: Hands-on: Build a GenAI Chatbot with Amazon Q for Business
location: 501 E Kennedy Blvd, Tampa, FL
description: Build a conversational AI chatbot with Amazon Q for Business. A hands-on workshop teaching generative AI chatbot development, including natural language understanding and human-like response generation. Speaker: Chuck Paquette, AWS Senior TAM.

title: Automate High-Scale Performance Testing for Resilient Web Applications on AWS
location: Online
description: Learn how to automate high-scale, multi-region performance testing using Distributed Load Testing on AWS, Amazon ECS on Fargate, and custom JMeter scripts to proactively identify performance bottlenecks.

title: Introducing Amazon Q: The Future of GenAI Assistance
location: 501 E Kennedy Blvd, Tampa, FL
description: A hands-on session exploring Amazon Q Business and Amazon Q Developer — covering code generation, debugging, testing, and enterprise data connectivity. Sponsored by Bespin Global.

title: Real World GenAI: Delivery Logistics with Amazon Bedrock and AWS Copilot
location: FIVE Labs, 4115 W Spruce St, Tampa, FL
description: RevStar presents a real-world GenAI solution for last-mile prescription delivery built on AWS Bedrock, covering rapid prototyping, dispatching copilot development, and logistics implications.

title: AWS Social Networking Event
location: Del Frisco's Grille, 4138 W Boy Scout Blvd, Tampa, FL
description: Annual networking event at Del Frisco's rooftop bar with free food and drinks. Featuring sponsors CloudSaver, JetSweep, and the non-profit H.U.G. Reading Program, plus prize raffles.

title: Accelerate Troubleshooting with AWS Observability
location: 501 E Kennedy Blvd, Tampa, FL
description: Mengdi Chen and Elamaran Shanmugam (both Tampa locals) reprise their re:Invent talk covering the AWS observability suite: CloudWatch, X-Ray, Grafana, Prometheus, OpenTelemetry, and cross-account observability.

title: AWS Security Posture Tool Deep Dive and Shootout
location: Kforce, 1150 Assembly Dr Suite 500, Tampa, FL
description: A comparison of cloud security posture management (CSPM) solutions including AWS native services, open-source tools, and Wiz. Also covers creating a 'vulnerable by design' AWS account for security skill-building.

title: AWS Customer Case Study: The Walt Disney Company
location: Entrepreneur Collaborative Center, 2101 E Palm Ave, Tampa, FL
description: Michael Berlet (Disney DPEP) presents behind-the-scenes automation using AWS Lambda, EC2, ECS, Selenium, WorkFusion, and UiPath for booking automation, personnel reassignment, and marketing campaign validation. Sponsored by TEKsystems.

title: AWS re:Invent Roadshow (Tampa)
location: Seminole Hard Rock Hotel & Casino Tampa, 5223 Orient Rd, Tampa, FL
description: AWS traveling re:Invent recap event at the Hard Rock. RSVPs redirected to the official AWS event page.

title: How Companies Can Build Products with Generative AI on AWS
location: Online
description: Panel discussion on how GenAI can transform business, and a how-to on building applications using Amazon Bedrock. Giveaway: 5 copies of 'Generative AI on AWS' (O'Reilly). Sponsored by Couchbase.

title: AWS Social Networking Event
location: Del Frisco's Grille, 4138 W Boy Scout Blvd, Tampa, FL
description: 6th anniversary celebration for the Tampa Bay AWS User Group at Del Frisco's rooftop bar. Free food and drinks. Sponsors: SUSE, CloudSaver, Check Point, Couchbase, JetSweep, Dexian.

title: Generative AI on AWS
location: Entrepreneur Collaborative Center, 2101 E Palm Ave, Tampa, FL
description: How to develop a generative AI strategy for your business and learn about the tools and services powering AWS AI, with live demos. Sponsored by Sela.

title: Java for Serverless Cloud Functions (AWS Lambda)
location: 1150 Assembly Dr Suite 500, Tampa, FL
description: An exploration of modern Java for serverless development, covering JVM advances (CRaC), low-overhead frameworks, AOT compilation, and live demos of Java Cloud Functions on AWS, Azure, and GCP.

title: AWS Customer Day: Event Driven Architectures
location: WeWork, 501 E Kennedy Blvd Suite 1400, Tampa, FL
description: Full-day AWS training event on serverless event-driven architectures using Amazon EventBridge, AWS Step Functions, and Lambda. Includes the Serverlesspresso workshop and networking happy hour. Invite only.

title: Introduction to AWS
location: 501 E Kennedy Blvd, Tampa, FL
description: A 101-level talk by Sam Kasimalla covering AWS core services: networking (VPC, Route53), compute (EC2), storage (S3, EBS, EFS), databases (RDS, DynamoDB), and containerization (EKS, ECS).

title: Cloud Encounters of the Ransomware Kind - Incident Response Experiences
location: 501 E Kennedy Blvd, Tampa, FL
description: GreenPages shares incident response lessons from cloud compromises, followed by Orca Security (AWS Security Partner of the Year 2022) presenting their Cloud Security Platform.

title: Artificial Intelligence / Machine Learning on AWS
location: Muma College of Business, 4202 E Fowler Ave, Tampa, FL
description: Quantiphi (AWS AIML Partner of the Year 2022) presents AI/ML use cases including intelligent document processing, computer vision, NLP, and predictive maintenance. Hosted by USF.

title: AWS FinOps - Best Practices for Optimizing Your Spend
location: Embarc Collective, 802 E Whiting St, Tampa, FL
description: Adam Richter, AWS Cost Efficiency and FinOps Specialist, presents best practices for cost-optimizing AWS environments including native tools, techniques, and key concepts. Sponsored by JFrog.

title: AWS Builder Workshop - Serverless Computing
location: 501 E Kennedy Blvd, Tampa, FL
description: Hands-on workshop building a serverless event-driven architecture using AWS API Gateway, Step Functions, EventBridge, Lambda, DynamoDB, and SNS. Temp AWS accounts provided.

title: AWS Social Networking Event to Kick Off 2023
location: 4138 W Boy Scout Blvd, Tampa, FL
description: Kickoff networking event for 2023. Sponsors: Arctic Wolf, GreenPages, RealCloud, HYCU, Check Point Software, and Axonius.

title: AWS re:Invent Recap in Tampa
location: 4221 W Boy Scout Blvd, Tampa, FL
description: Annual re:Invent recap with local AWS Solution Architects discussing top announcements from the 2022 conference. Hosted by Gerdau.

title: AWS Security: Use Cases, Real Experiences, and Challenges
location: 615 Channelside Dr, Tampa, FL
description: Rolando Panez (Check Point Software) covers DevSecOps concepts, cloud-native application attack surfaces, and security best practices from real customer engagements.

title: Securing Workloads, Applications, and Containers on AWS
location: 501 E Kennedy Blvd, Tampa, FL
description: Matthew Chapman (Trend Micro) discusses current trends in securing workloads, applications, and containers on AWS, and how DevOps should integrate with your security posture.

title: Securing AWS - Presented by Orca Security
location: 501 E Kennedy Blvd, Tampa, FL
description: Orca Security discusses the state of AWS security and how to better protect cloud environments from internal and external threats, with a focus on context-driven alert prioritization. Sponsored by Presidio.

title: January AWS Social Reception
location: Del Frisco's Grille (Rooftop), 4138 W Boy Scout Blvd, Tampa, FL
description: Social reception with AWS solution architects and community members. No presentations — just networking. Food and drinks provided by Trend Micro.

title: re:Invent 2021 Recap
location: 501 E Kennedy Blvd, Tampa, FL
description: First in-person event in 18 months. Ayo Adegoke (AWS Solutions Architect) recaps re:Invent 2021 new services and features. Prizes including an ugliest holiday sweater contest. Hosted by Jefferson Frank.

title: AWS IoT for Developers: Amazon Kinesis Video Streams with WebRTC
location: Online
description: David Malone (AWS IoT Solutions Architect) demonstrates building end-to-end IoT applications using Raspberry Pi, provisioning IoT Things, and integrating live video feeds into Amazon Kinesis Video Streams.

title: Network Connectivity in AWS: VPC Communications & Workload Acceleration
location: Online
description: David Gendel (AWS Solutions Architect Manager) covers VPC Peering, Transit Gateway, CloudFront, Global Accelerator, and VPN Accelerator best practices.

title: Building Cloud-Native Mobile Applications with Carlos Souza
location: Online
description: Lightning talk on how AWS Amplify can help mobile projects launch faster and deliver real customer value. Speaker: Carlos Souza, full-stack engineer and Pluralsight author.

title: AWS Observability Services
location: Online
description: Imaya Kumar Jagannathan (AWS Senior Solutions Architect) covers CloudWatch, X-Ray, Amazon Managed Service for Prometheus, Amazon Managed Grafana, and AWS Distro for OpenTelemetry.

title: Proactive Programming using AWS Services
location: Online
description: PJ Hagerty (Senior Developer Advocate, Mattermost) discusses methods for ensuring code works correctly before deploying to production using the feedback loop and AWS services.

title: Creating Secure Landing Zones in AWS
location: Online
description: Webinar on baking governance and security into AWS landing zones using Control Tower, AWS Organizations, automation, and native AWS security services. Panel: AWS and GreenPages.

title: Amazon re:Invent 2020 Recap
location: Online
description: Trevor Roberts (AWS Sr. Solutions Architect) reviews announcements from re:Invent 2020. Sponsored by iO Associates.

title: Using DynamoDB in Serverless Applications
location: Online
description: Alex DeBrie (AWS Data Hero, author of 'The DynamoDB Book') covers why DynamoDB is ideal for serverless, how to get started, and data modeling best practices. Sponsored by iO Associates.

title: AWS Data Lakes
location: Online
description: Austin Loveless (AWS Community Hero) covers architecting a data lake on AWS using S3, Athena, and Glue. Sponsored by iO Associates.

title: Introduction to Machine Learning on AWS
location: Online
description: Shashank Prasanna (AWS ML Developer Advocate) introduces AutoML using Amazon SageMaker AutoPilot and AutoGluon. No ML expertise required. Sponsored by iO Associates.

title: AWS Automation: Moving from CloudFormation Templates to Terraform
location: Online
description: Divya Singh (Senior Software Engineer, Nielsen) shares how a multi-billion dollar company successfully transitioned from CloudFormation to Terraform and the key advantages gained.

title: AWS Success Story and Security Deep Dive
location: Jefferson Frank, 501 E Kennedy Blvd Unit 1600, Tampa, FL
description: BankUnited VP of Cloud Michael Lehmbeck shares their AWS journey for disaster recovery and mobile banking. Wayne Davis (AWS) covers GuardDuty, Inspector, and Amazon Detective. Sponsored by Jefferson Frank.

title: AWS re:Invent Recap
location: SOFWERX, 1925 E 2nd Ave #102, Tampa, FL
description: Alejandra Novack (AWS Developer Advocate) reviews announcements from re:Invent 2019. Sponsored by Jefferson Frank and GreenPages, hosted by SOFWERX.

title: Architecting a Serverless, Event Driven SaaS Platform
location: Kforce, 1150 Assembly Dr Suite 500, Tampa, FL
description: MontyCloud's Director of Engineering and CEO present how they built a modern Cloud IT Services Management platform using a serverless, event-driven architecture.

title: AWS Well-Architected: A Blueprint for Cloud Success
location: Jefferson Frank, 501 E Kennedy Blvd Unit 1600, Tampa, FL
description: Jeff Fudge (GreenPages) covers the five pillars of the AWS Well-Architected Framework: operational excellence, security, reliability, performance efficiency, and cost optimization.

title: AI and Machine Learning on AWS
location: Vaco, 4030 W Boy Scout Blvd #100, Tampa, FL
description: Tristan Li (AWS Solutions Architect) presents the latest AI and ML features on the AWS platform. Includes a $250 AWS usage credit raffle. Sponsored and hosted by VACO.

title: AWS, Windows and .NET - Everything You Need To Know
location: The Undercroft, 1320 E. 9th Ave, Tampa, FL
description: Dmitry Kagansky (AWS Senior Solutions Architect, Microsoft Platform) deep dives into migrating, managing, and developing Microsoft applications on AWS including landing zones and architecture patterns.

title: What, Why and Tools for Infrastructure as Code (IaC)
location: Keiser University Tampa, 5002 West Waters Avenue, Tampa, FL
description: Chuck Griess (DXC) explains Infrastructure as Code, why companies should adopt it for AWS, surveys available tools, and demos Terraform for provisioning, modifying, and removing servers. Sponsored by Criterion Executive Search.

title: AWS Security Controls and Auditing
location: Cousins Conference Center, 4221 W Boy Scout Blvd Suite 140, Tampa, FL
description: Jeremy Rasmussen (CTO, Abacode) introduces AWS security and monitoring capabilities, vulnerability scanning and assessments, and IoT environment security and auditing.

title: re:Invent 2018 Recap
location: Feather Sound Country Club, 2201 Feather Sound Dr, Clearwater, FL
description: An AWS Solutions Architect presents a recap of re:Invent 2018 announcements. Sponsored by Accenture (AWS Premier Consulting Partner) and held at Feather Sound Country Club.

title: User Case Study - Accusoft SaaS Product on AWS
location: Accusoft, 829 Martin Luther King Jr Blvd, Tampa, FL
description: Accusoft presents how they use CloudFormation, CloudWatch, EC2 Auto Scaling, and load balancing for their PrizmDoc Cloud product, with plans to migrate to Kubernetes on AWS.

title: Customer Case Study - Verizon and Amazon GuardDuty
location: Verizon, 7701 Telecom Parkway, Temple Terrace, FL
description: Doug Woolley (Verizon) presents the CAMP cloud migration portal and Verizon's serverless hackathon. Cameron Worrell (AWS Solutions Architect) demos GuardDuty and AWS WAF for automatic host blocking. Sponsored by Sirius Computer Solutions.

title: Customer Case Study - Malwarebytes
location: Malwarebytes Clearwater Office, 5739 Rio Vista Drive, Clearwater, FL
description: Malwarebytes engineers deep dive into their big data platform on AWS that ingests and processes billions of events daily in real time, covering stream ingestion and analytics architecture.

title: Building a Serverless Website
location: Nielsen, 501 Brooker Creek Boulevard, Oldsmar, FL
description: David Malone (AWS Solutions Architect) live-builds a serverless website using CloudFront, Lambda@Edge, Cognito, S3, Lambda, DynamoDB, and React, covering continuous delivery best practices.

title: How to Achieve a Quick and Successful Cloud Migration
location: Kforce, 1001 E Palm Ave, Tampa, FL
description: Kevin Downs (New Relic) and Brad Edwards (Relus Cloud) cover cloud migration concepts, success metrics, and how the New Relic Digital Intelligence Platform provides quantifiable migration results.

title: AWS Elasticity and Auto-Scaling
location: Keiser University Tampa, 5002 West Waters Avenue, Tampa, FL
description: GreenPages architects David Jones and Chris Williams live-demo a multi-AZ web server/RDS architecture with Auto Scale Groups, triggering ASG events and demonstrating high availability.

title: Machine Learning (ML) on AWS
location: Keiser University Tampa, 5002 West Waters Avenue, Tampa, FL
description: Tristan Li (AWS Enterprise Solutions Architect) covers ML on AWS for predictive analytics. Meryem Berrada (Nielsen) discusses how Nielsen uses Alexa, DeepLens, and SageMaker to capture and analyze viewing data. Sponsored by Eliassen Group.

title: AWS re:Invent 2017 Recap
location: Nielsen, 501 Brooker Creek Boulevard, Oldsmar, FL
description: Ryan Boyer (AWS Partner Solutions Architect) recaps exciting announcements and feature enhancements from re:Invent 2017. Hosted by Nielsen's Global Technology and Innovation Center.

title: November Social Event
location: World of Beer, 5311 Avion Park Drive, Tampa, FL
description: First-year anniversary celebration for the Tampa Bay AWS User Group. Networking with cloud peers, free drinks, no presentations. Sponsored by SHI.

title: .NET on AWS, IoT and Lambda
location: e-merge Print Management, 5100 W Cypress St., Tampa, FL
description: Chad Lacy (Amazon) presents .NET and Microsoft workloads on AWS. e-merge Print Management presents their AWS IoT, Lambda, and RDS workload with the full engineering team.

title: Avoiding Cloud Jail and Controlling AWS Spend
location: Concerto Cloud Services, 4830 W. Kennedy Blvd, Tampa, FL
description: Concerto Cloud Services presents a customer use case on avoiding runaway cloud costs and managing AWS spend, with a tour of their new headquarters.

title: Serverless Computing (Lambda)
location: Iron Yard, 260 1st Ave S #300, St. Petersburg, FL
description: Use case presentations: 'Practical Lambda and Beyond' by Danny Murawsky (Urgos.io) and advanced Lambda tips, tricks, and live examples by the Malwarebytes engineering team. Sponsored by Sirius Computer Solutions.

title: Customer Use Case - Governance and Security at Verizon
location: Verizon, 7701 Telecom Parkway, Temple Terrace, FL
description: Lee Ziliak and Chitra Prakash (Verizon) discuss cloud governance and operations. Eric Rose (Amazon) covers AWS security. Includes $50 AWS certificate giveaways.

title: Happy Hour Social
location: Kona Grill, 4134 West Boy Scout Boulevard, Tampa, FL
description: Networking event sponsored by Concerto Cloud Services with giveaways, raffle prizes, and a complimentary drink.

title: Taming the Cloud at Scale
location: Malwarebytes Office, 410 Park Place Blvd Suite #200, Clearwater, FL
description: Malwarebytes SRE team presents on Elastic Beanstalk, Lambda, and ECS with alternatives to CloudFormation. Amazon Echo raffle included.

title: Happy Hour Social
location: The Patio Tampa, 421 S MacDill Ave, Tampa, FL
description: Networking happy hour for the local AWS community with free appetizers and a round of drinks.

title: Inaugural Meetup: Member Intros, Q&A, and Customer Use Case
location: Sogeti Tampa, 1000 N Ashley Dr #650, Tampa, FL
description: The first Tampa Bay AWS User Group meeting. Member introductions, open Q&A, agenda shaping, and a customer use case presentation from Digital Media Solutions.
